title: Pragmata's tale of AI slop, humanity, & lunar conquest makes it the timeliest sci-fi game of the year
summary: Capcom's new sci-fi game 'Pragmata' explores themes of AI, humanity, and lunar conquest, blending nostalgia with timely storytelling. The game is praised for its optimistic and well-timed narrative amid current technological and space industry trends.

title: Artemis 2 and Tiangong space station astronauts set record for farthest distance between humans
summary: On April 6, the Artemis 2 astronauts and Tiangong space station crew set a record for the farthest distance between humans in space. This milestone highlights growing international activity in human spaceflight.
company: NASA and China Manned Space Agency

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 206 — I Want to Believe
summary: This Week In Space podcast Episode 206 discusses the implications of discovering alien life, featuring Dr. Brianne Suldovsky from Portland State University as a guest.

title: Don't miss the moon, Venus and the Pleiades align after sunset on April 19
summary: A celestial alignment of the moon, Venus, and the Pleiades star cluster will be visible after sunset on April 19, with the crescent moon also displaying earthshine. This astronomical event offers a rare opportunity to observe these celestial bodies in close proximity.

title: This life-hunting rover may be SpaceX's 1st-ever Mars launch
summary: SpaceX will launch Europe's Rosalind Franklin rover to Mars in 2028, but not using its Starship rocket. The mission aims to search for signs of past or present life on Mars.

title: Watch live: Blue Origin reusing New Glenn rocket stage for 1st time on April 19 launch
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket is scheduled for its third launch on April 19, featuring a reused first-stage booster for the first time. The event will be livestreamed for public viewing.

title: 'Tall waves moving in slow motion': Here's how oily oceans on Saturn's giant moon Titan may behave
summary: Scientists study wave behavior on Titan, Saturn's moon, where oily oceans exhibit slow-motion waves influenced by liquid properties and gravity. The research highlights how wave dynamics differ from Earth's oceans.

title: CSDA Quality Assessment Report Evaluates Satellogic NewSat Data
summary: The CSDA Quality Assessment Report has evaluated Satellogic's NewSat data, highlighting its contributions to Earth science research and applications. This adds to the growing documentation on the value of commercial data in scientific studies.

title: Webinar 4/29: NASA CSDA Program Vendor Focus- MDA Space
summary: NASA's CSDA Program will host a vendor focus webinar featuring MDA Space on April 29 at 2:00 p.m. EDT. The event aims to provide insights into the CSDA program and MDA Space's role.

title: NASA Artemis II Human Research Data Methodology Challenge
summary: NASA's Human Research Program (HRP) is developing methods to safeguard astronaut health for long-term missions on the Moon and Mars using ground facilities, the ISS, and analog environments. The Artemis II mission was the first major step in this research initiative.

title: NASA, OPM Announce New NASA Force Website, Open Job Applications
summary: NASA and the U.S. Office of Personnel Management launched the NASA Force website to recruit engineers and technologists for mission-critical roles in NASA's exploration and research programs. The initiative aims to place high-impact technical talent into key positions supporting America's air and space efforts.

title: Quality Assessment Report Evaluates Tomorrow.io Precipitation Radar Data
summary: A quality assessment report has evaluated the precipitation radar data from Tomorrow.io, highlighting its contributions to Earth science research and applications. This adds to the growing evidence of commercial data's value in scientific studies.

title: Volunteers Discover Rare Space Weather Events Using Their Ears
summary: Volunteers are helping scientists analyze rare space weather events by listening to data sonified from NASA's HARP project, which compares Earth's magnetic field to a 'giant harp in space.' This citizen science initiative aids in understanding the behavior of these waves.

title: NASA’s SPHEREx Observatory Maps Interstellar Ice in Milky Way
summary: NASA's SPHEREx Observatory has mapped interstellar ice and polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons in the Milky Way's Cygnus X region. This discovery highlights the chemical composition of one of the galaxy's most active star-forming areas.

title: NASA Invites Media to Latvia Artemis Accords Signing Ceremony
summary: Latvia is set to sign the Artemis Accords in a ceremony hosted by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman at NASA Headquarters. The event will include Latvia's minister for education and science and the chargé d’affaires at the Embassy of the Republic of Latvia.

title: At the Edge of Light
summary: A photograph taken on April 6, 2026, captures the Moon's far side near the terminator, highlighting long shadows and the Orientale Basin. The image provides a detailed view of the lunar surface under low-angle sunlight.

title: New Glenn set to launch on third mission, reuse booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket is preparing for its third mission, marking its first attempt to reuse a booster. The launch follows its second mission just five months prior.

title: Vast unveils Astronaut Flight Suit and revolutionary Large Docking Adapter
summary: Vast introduced an astronaut flight suit and a large docking adapter, showcasing a blend of fashion and functionality. This innovation supports the development of their commercial space station.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have completed full engine static fire tests as part of the Artemis program. This milestone follows the successful return of the Orion spacecraft.

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew members conducted a spacewalk and managed vehicle departures aboard the International Space Station in March. This marked another active month for the ISS operations.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will launch with a previously flown booster. Six launches are scheduled worldwide this week.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent Cygnus S.S. William McCool mission.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. This marks a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than any previous crewed mission. The mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has debuted new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing and outlining future deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making refinements at Starbase Pad 2 in preparation for the return of Booster 19. The updates are part of ongoing preparations at SpaceX's facility in Texas.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned in a space industry news context, but no specific details about the news are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores ESA's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, held on 8-9 June 2026 in Paris, France, as part of the SME and Midcap Days. An online preparatory forum will take place in April and May.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: The article discusses the successful transmission of Celeste's first navigation signal. The location for this milestone is highlighted as ideal.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 satellites have created 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission reveals solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously thought.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, scheduled for 16-17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improvements in attitude control systems for satellites or probes. This innovation could enhance precision in navigation and mission operations.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light propulsion using graphene aerogels in microgravity during ESA's parabolic flight campaign, showing potential for satellite maneuvering. The study, led by ULB and Khalifa University, found that lasers could instantly propel the aerogels, with thrust controllable by laser intensity.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Graphene and lasers could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment on a gravity rollercoaster demonstrated this potential.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A breakthrough silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space electronics. This innovation could enhance satellite and spacecraft capabilities.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Thruster Market with Gauss
summary: Rocket Lab is expanding into the thruster market through its new Gauss division, aiming to vertically integrate and address scalability concerns for customers. The move is part of Rocket Lab's strategy to become a key supplier in the space industry.

title: Axiom Space’s Suit Set to Fly in 2027
summary: Axiom Space is preparing to fly its first qualification spacesuit in 2027, as confirmed by Chief Astronaut Michael López-Alegría. The company is advancing suit production despite external uncertainties.

title: Voyager Technologies Wins Its First Private ISS Mission
summary: Voyager Technologies has secured its first private mission to the International Space Station (ISS) in 2026, marking a shift as new players enter the competition previously dominated by Axiom Space.

title: Seraphim Space Aims To Shape Strategy With New Advisory Council
summary: Seraphim Space has formed a new advisory council to address key challenges in the space industry, including sustainability, regulation, national security, and international collaboration. The council aims to shape strategic decisions for the company.

title: ORBX ETF Launches Amid Wave of Space Tech IPOs
summary: The ORBX ETF has been launched to track companies generating over half their revenue from space technology across sectors like transportation, software, and satellite data services. This launch coincides with a surge in space tech IPOs.

title: Kepler Leads Team to Test ESA’s ‘Fiber in the Sky’
summary: ESA has selected a team led by Kepler Communications to test its 'fiber in the sky' HydRON orbital communications network. The initiative aims to advance space-based high-speed data transmission.

title: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman On Artemis, Budget, And Establishing a Lasting Space Vision
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman discusses the Artemis program, budget challenges, and the need for a sustainable space vision. He highlights the importance of balancing scientific passion with financial planning.

title: CLD Companies Say NASA Is Wrong. NASA Says Prove It.
summary: CLD companies challenge NASA's claims, with NASA responding by asking for proof. Isaacman, representing CLD providers, expresses skepticism about NASA's statements.

title: Slingshot Introduces AI-Powered Portal Platform
summary: Slingshot Aerospace launched an AI-powered portal platform to help operators detect anomalies and make decisions efficiently. The platform consolidates multiple functions into a single interface for streamlined operations.

title: ICON Prime Launches, Led By Former TX Rep
summary: ICON Prime has been launched as a new business unit by ICON, a robotic construction company, with leadership from a former Texas representative. The unit will collaborate with the Pentagon and NASA.

title: IFC News Roundup from AIX
summary: The AIX Aircraft Interiors Expo provided a platform for satellite companies to advocate for in-flight connectivity solutions to airlines. Via Satellite highlighted key announcements related to this sector.

title: Airlines See IFC as Way to Build Relationships With Customers
summary: Airlines are leveraging in-flight connectivity (IFC) to enhance customer relationships as it becomes a core part of the flying experience. The focus on IFC is highlighted in a recent industry discussion.

title: NorthStar Earth & Space to Go Public in SPAC Deal at $300M Valuation
summary: NorthStar Earth & Space plans to go public via a SPAC deal valued at $300 million. The Canadian space domain awareness company integrates ground-based systems for its operations.

title: ESA Taps Kepler to Lead Next Stage of HydRON Optical Program
summary: The European Space Agency has selected Kepler Communications as the prime contractor for HydRON Element 3, the next phase of a sovereign optical communications constellation. This marks a significant step in advancing optical communication technology for space.

title: Space Force, NATO Commanders Share Threat Perspectives
summary: U.S. Space Force is actively identifying new space capabilities and managing threats from adversaries while creating missions to defend assets. NATO commanders share perspectives on space security threats.

title: Air Force Awarded Development Contract for Space-Based AMTI; Meink Says Risk Low
summary: The U.S. Department of the Air Force awarded a development contract for space-based air moving target indication (AMTI) to multiple vendors. Officials indicated that the program carries low risk.

title: Boeing and Millennium Space Systems Add Mid-Class Resolute Satellite Bus
summary: Boeing and its subsidiary Millennium Space Systems have introduced a new mid-class satellite platform called Resolute, offering enhanced capabilities compared to traditional small satellites. The Resolute bus is designed to provide increased performance for space missions.

title: Slingshot Aerospace Releases AI-Powered Analytics Platform for Space Operations
summary: Slingshot Aerospace launched Portal, an AI-powered analytics platform for satellite data monitoring and analysis. The software integrates Slingshot’s data flows with external sources to enhance space operations.

title: Industry Builds the Case Around Architecture Options for Orbital Data Centers
summary: The space industry is exploring orbital data centers as launch costs decrease and demand for alternatives to terrestrial data centers grows. This shift aims to provide new infrastructure solutions in space.

title: Transport Layer of PWSA Will Eventually Transition From SDA to New Acquisition Executive, Sandhoo Says
summary: The Space Force plans to transition the communications layer of the Space Development Agency's Proliferated Warfighter Space Architecture (PWSA) from SDA to a new acquisition executive. This move is part of the Space Force's broader rollout of a new acquisition structure.
